results.rendered_html: vyāpaka (von āp mit vi) adj. (f. vyāpikā) durchdringend, sich weithin erstreckend, allgemein verbreitet; in der Logik stets enthalten in, inhärirend (Feuer z. B. ist vyāpaka, Rauch vyāpya)  SUŚR. 1,363,4 . puruṣa  KAṬHOP. 6,8 .  MBH. 12,7400 . sarvatra vyāpikā  BRAHMAVAIV. P. _im_ ŚKDR.  tiryagūrdhvamadhastācca vyāpako mahimā hareḥ  KUMĀRAS. 6,71 .  MĀRK. P. 46,16 .  BHĀG. P. 7,6,22 . 7,19 .  Verz. d. Oxf. H. 104,b,35 . 37 .  TARKAS. 45 . 52 .  BHĀṢĀP. 137 . Schol. zu  KAP. 1,101 . 139 .  KUSUM. 19,1 . Schol. zu  P. 2,1,57 . mantreṇa vyāpakaṃ (sc. nyāsaṃ) kṛtvā so v. a. über den ganzen Körper auftragen  PAÑCAR. 3,15,18. fg.  Davon nom. abstr. ˚tā f.  BHĀG. P. 4,28,40 .  TARKAS. 46 . ˚tva n. 45 .  ŚĀṆḌ. 87 .  BHĀṢĀP. 9 .  Z. d. d. m. G. 6,26, N. 1 .

results.rendered_html:
vyāpaka a. (pikā f.)
1 Pervading, comprehensive, diffusive, widely spread, extending over the whole of anything; tiryagūrdhvamadhastācca vyāpako mahimā hareḥ  Ku. 6. 71. 
2 (In law) Comprehending all the points of an argument.
3 Invariably concomitant.
4 That which is more extensive than the vyāpya; e. g. in the instance manuṣyo martyaḥ; martyaṃ is vyāpaka as it includes manuṣya, and is more extensive than it.
— kaḥ An attribute which is invariably concomitant or inherent.
— kaṃ An invariably concomitant or inherent property.
